reviews.review_title: Great stuff, but new regression bug
reviews.review_description: i can no longer do transforms on my matrix. I can do translations but changes in z are no longer possible, and rotations and scaling no longer stick. There also doesn't seem to be any reliable way of doing these operations with hand tracking, and as far as I can tell there is no hand tracking configuration for node expand and collapse. I would love to be able to use it completely via hand tracking for the full Tony Stark experience but it's not quite there yet.
This app is a dream for ADHD coming true. I love it

It helps me mindmap my goals in the best way possible. I find it very relaxing… I don’t know why, I guess the music and scenery put me in the zone, it’s almost meditative. Some minor improvements could be made like perhaps you could make it easier to download images from sources other than a search engine (like Google Drive), as I have not had any success doing that. Another thing I find mildly annoying is the app automatically capitalizes the first letter of every word I type and some random things are missing like the dollar sign on the keyboard. Other than that, totally recommended! It is an overall fantastic app that helps me organize my life and breathe a little easier.

reviews.review_title: Great App, lack of Updates (as of today)
reviews.review_description:
One thing I practice before purchasing the ‘’Pro’’ version of an App is checking how many updates and how often the company does them. I dont see any with NODA, I love the App but some times my Mind Map moves to a strange arrangement and I can’t click Undo or bring it back to where it was. The interface needs minor corrections. 

This is The BEST mind map app that exists, and I really hope they can communicate how often they will keep making it better, so I can purchase the paid version…

I use Noda every day. It's easily the most awesome, intuitive and well thought out app I've used. I'm a big picture thinker so being able to plan using Noda is a Godsend. I use it to create maps for self help books I'm reading, plan out my life, aspirations, or sometimes just to have fun creating comic strips using AR generated images and text. The possibilities are absolute huge and I pray they keep on developing this beautiful piece of software. I subscribe monthly so I get a few extra locations. Please add more locations guys as this would be amazing. Thanks to all of you

Hello, I originally wanted to purchase the notice subscription where it would be giving me the CSV import and I opted for the trial version and either due to a bug - or if it was purposeful or a marketing tactic, but I have to purchase the core in order to upload a CSV, which is not the case why I originally even opted for the trial version

reviews.review_title: Love it but it needs a bit more work
reviews.review_description:
I do enjoy everything so far. But, I don't know how to page up when on the web. And the keyboard isn't complete. 
When I went to my chatgpt account I çouldnt scroll through to finish what I was done. But I loved the fact I could drag off what could be seen on the screen.. and hope they finish the keyboard properly. But otherwise fantastic...!!!!!!

I hadn't fully considered how much better brainstorming would be in a VR medium instead of 2D, but it's definitely impressed me. It's incredibly easy to use & load previous sessions. It has loads of simple features to make clean & easy-to-follow Mind Maps, and I'm excited to see the features coming soon!

reviews.review_title: VR Mind Mapping is a Game Changer
reviews.review_description: I have used many 2D mind mapping apps and never stuck with them. Being able to walk around in a virtual space filled with ideas really works for me. I feel it improves my memory and allows me to reveal hidden connections in my notes. I am a happy subscriber!

😖👎 I would want to give this program a five stars because for use for this and functionability it deserves it. But because of the developers greed and insistance upon an inane monthly subscription it only deserves a one-star review or less. I could certainly afford with their charging if I wanted to ...But I also could afford to pay $50 for a Big Mac at McDonald's for that matter. I simply refuse to be exploited 💰💰💰 at any time by anybody for any reason.
      Regardless of what justification developers may use for this ridiculous subscription model I can absolutely assure you and guarantee you that somebody else will come out with a similar or even better mind map that is either free or has a one-time purchase price.

And when they do it is going to blow this one out the water and make it obsolete. This subscription is an extremely poor business decision and unless the developers are content to remain low value and never reach the Pinnacle of success they would never be a successful as they could be if they would adapt a more reasonable model.

And since so many people have downgraded this app for the same reason and I voice their concerns it is looking like they just don't give a d@mn how the actual users feel about it. 

This indifference is a slap in the face to those of us who have the power to either make you successful ... Or not. 

Other delivers developers at least have the dignity and class to respond to reviews whether they're negative or positive. 

So like they say on the Shark Tank 🦈 ...I'M OUT!!! 👎

developer_response.body:
Hello! It has been some time and I do want to apologize that Noda launched with only a limited free version and a subscription. It wasn't the right mix to meet everyone's needs. 

There is now a one-time purchase option for access to the app with some limits on the ongoing cloud services. We've also improved the free version.

We'd love to know what you think of the app these days and would greatly appreciate if you could give it another look!

reviews.review_description:
The full version for the Rift was a one time fee and i was ok with that. Actually was excited when noda was announced for the Quest so i could use it without Air Link e.g. at work.
But now they charge 60 €/year for basically the same app. That is not worth it.

Would've bought the Quest version but i won't spend any money on a subscription.

developer_response.body: Hello. Thank you for your comment. There are some cloud services in the Quest version and more on the way that make subscription the only sustainable option. But thanks to your feedback we realize that some people would be happier with a one-time price with limited use of these services. We have split out the features to make it possible and now offer a one-time purchase option similar to PC. We hope this change addresses your concern and if you agree please consider updating your rating. Happy to hear your thoughts any time, -Team Noda

reviews.review_title: Subscription. No way. We're not idiots!
reviews.review_description:
I think its LOUD and CLEAR from other people that have bothered to review this App that if the Developers continues to try and push the paid subscription then this will be dead in the water before it even begins. 

Now I did read the excuse that the "voice option is metered" which is why they need the money and frankly I think that this is one of the WEAKEST excuses I've heard in a long time.. Find a work around if its genuinely true. Personally I think it sounds like Bull****. 

So to the developers: You have an interesting product with potential but SUBSCRIPTION is a NO NO... Everyone is saying it!!! Anyone actually dumb enough to pay for the SUBSCRIPTION probably needs this app just to work out how stupid they actually are. 

Developers pay attention NO TO SUBSCRIPTION. It's just Greedy and EVERYONE is thinking it. A one off payment I would consider although it needs improvement and further options.

reviews.review_title: Its a nice idea but not worth the cost
reviews.review_description: Its a 3d brainstorming app that thinks its worth $60 a year.  No thanks ill stick with graph  paper.

reviews.review_title: Wrong info on App pricing, bad bad
reviews.review_description:
Good enough idea, needs developing, more choices for each node needed.
BUT PRICE IN APP IT SAYS £0.01A MONTH TRIED TO BUY THAT THEN BILL ON OCULUS WAS £3.99 A MONTH. NEEDLESS TO SAY WHICH I PREFER, AND HOW DISAPPOINTED I AM.

developer_response.body:
Very sorry about that! There was a problem with the reported prices from the store. The pricing info has been removed until it's fixed.

We're working on more choices for the nodes (shape, color, pattern, etc.), good call. Hope to have you with us in future.

reviews.review_description:
This app is the future and the best mind mapping tool out there. For visual thinkers, this will accelerate your ideas or projects. It is my favorite oculus tool for mind mapping. The ui, ambient music, and and ability to move on way that is realistic,
Makes this ahead of others. The subscription price  is a steal compared to other mind mapping tools.
Add this to your stack, and let your ideas flourish.

10/10
Awesomeness

I am using this app for some software development projects, and the subscription is worth it if you are familiar with programming. I love the import/export feature and have been able to make some cool structures out of CSV files. The new Integration API has a lot of potential to make an app within the app. Really cool!

reviews.review_title: Subscription Model is a Big-Turn Off
reviews.review_description: I'm echoing what others have been saying about this app. I would gladly pay for a one-time fee but $60 per year?!!!

developer_response.body: Hello! Sorry to have missed the mark initially. We've updated the app to offer a one-time purchase option for the core features without the unlimited cloud services that are included in the subscription. We hope you'll find this fits your expectations and would be happy to have you take another look. Re-rating based on new information is also extremely appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
Noda helped me project my ideas and get a clear overview of them. I couldn't imagine a better tool to do that, although the tool could be better still. What I miss:
- The ability to add description to the nodes. Opening a node and navigating to the description won't do it. I would love to see the text floating or show when the node is pointed at.
- A better keyboard. To write a digit I have to change keyboard which switches beck after writing that digit
- Arrows and description for links

I would gladly pay a one-time fee for it, but never a subscription. Consider that plus maybe an additional fee for inviting others to your space.

reviews.review_title: Yeah subscription is a big nope
reviews.review_description:
If this was a one-time $30 app I'd give it 5 stars.

Dev is not cultivating community goodwill judging from these reviews.

developer_response.body: You are right the subscription model has been controversial. Many people who use the app heavily have told us it makes sense and they are happy with it and supporting the ongoing development but for others it doesn't match their usage plans or investment in the app. We've tried to balance this by splitting out the core features and offering a one-time purchase option. Please check this out and let us know how it looks to you. An updated review score would be sincerely appreciated!!
